ddls:
CREATE TABLE "systems" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the system (e.g., AA_ADT_EVM, AC_ADT_IN_COPY) */,
  "assigning_authorities" TEXT /* Assigning authorities and identifiers for the system */,
  "preferred_message" TEXT /* Preferred message type for sharing data */,
  "last_modifier" TEXT /* Name of the last person who modified the entry */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the system's purpose and functionality */,
  "status" TEXT /* The current status of the system (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address for contacting system administrators or support */,
  "technical_requirements" TEXT /* Technical requirements or specifications for integrating with the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "messages" (
  "message_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the system associated with this message */,
  "message_type" TEXT /* Type of the message (e.g., ADT, ACK) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the message structure */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the message type and its use */,
  "schema" TEXT /* The schema or structure of the message in a standardized format (e.g., JSON, XML) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("message_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system being accessed or modified */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* The status of the action (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number for user contact */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the system associated with this version */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the message associated with this version */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the version */,
  "changelog" TEXT /* A changelog detailing the changes made in the version */,
  "status" TEXT /* The status of the version (e.g., draft, released, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_message_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_id") REFERENCES messages ("message_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each action type */,
  "action_name" TEXT /* Name of the action */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the action entails */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the action (e.g., system, message) */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the action is critical and requires special attention */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Description of permissions associated with the role */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the role has administrative privileges */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the role is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_users" (
  "system_user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the system-user relationship */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system the user is associated with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the system */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* The level of access the user has to the system (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "is_owner" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user is the owner of the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_users_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_users_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES systems ("system_id")
)
